title: Multiply with 2 and 4
essential_question: What strategies can you use to multiply with 2 and 4?
strategies: To multiply by 2: double the number
To multiply by 4: double the double
Use an array to see the product

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.4 Lesson 4.1 'Multiply with 2 and 4' pp.139-142
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic D & Module 3 — Multiplying with Units of 2 and 4

pretest.question: There are 4 bags with 8 oranges each. How many oranges?

learn.title: Doubling Strategy for ×2
learn.content: Multiplying by 2 means doubling. 2 × 8 = 8 + 8 = 16. Think: 'What's the double of that number?'

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.4 Lesson 4.1 'Multiply with 2 and 4' pp.139-142
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic D & Module 3 — Multiplying with Units of 2 and 4
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Double the Double for ×4
learn.content: To multiply by 4, first multiply by 2, then double again. Example: 4 × 6 → 2×6=12, then 12×2=24. So 4×6=24.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.4 Lesson 4.1 'Multiply with 2 and 4' pp.139-142
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic D & Module 3 — Multiplying with Units of 2 and 4
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.4 Lesson 4.1 'Multiply with 2 and 4' pp.139-142
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic D & Module 3 — Multiplying with Units of 2 and 4
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Arrays Show ×2 and ×4
learn.content: An array is a neat grid of rows and columns. 2 × 5 means 2 rows of 5 — 10 dots in all. 4 × 5 means 4 rows of 5 — 20 dots. When you double the rows, you double the total.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.4 Lesson 4.1 'Multiply with 2 and 4' pp.139-142
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic D & Module 3 — Multiplying with Units of 2 and 4
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Build ×4 from ×2 You Know
learn.content: Every ×4 fact is a ×2 fact doubled. If 2 × 7 = 14, then 4 × 7 = 28. If 2 × 9 = 18, then 4 × 9 = 36. Once you have your doubles memorized, the fours come free.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.4 Lesson 4.1 'Multiply with 2 and 4' pp.139-142
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic D & Module 3 — Multiplying with Units of 2 and 4
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: ×2 = 더블 — '+2'가 아니다!
learn.content: ×2를 만나면 '+2'로 잘못 계산하지 마세요. ×2는 같은 수를 한 번 더 더하는 '더블(double)'입니다. 예: 7 × 2 = 7 + 7 = 14 (NOT 7 + 2 = 9). 흔한 실수: '×2'를 '+2'로 자동 변환 → 7 × 2 = 9. 기억법: '곱하기 2 = 자기 자신을 한 번 더'.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.4 Lesson 4.1 'Multiply with 2 and 4' pp.139-142
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic D & Module 3 — Multiplying with Units of 2 and 4
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: ×4 = 더블의 더블 — 두 단계로 풀기
learn.content: ×4 사실은 ×2를 두 번 한 것과 같습니다 (×4 = ×2 × ×2). 예: 6 × 4 = 6 × 2 × 2 = 12 × 2 = 24. 단계 1: 다른 인자를 더블 (6 → 12). 단계 2: 그 결과를 또 더블 (12 → 24). 장점: 외울 사실이 줄어듦. ×2만 잘 하면 ×4가 공짜로 따라옴.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.4 Lesson 4.1 'Multiply with 2 and 4' pp.139-142
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic D & Module 3 — Multiplying with Units of 2 and 4
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: ×2·×4 즉답 전략 요약
learn.content: 전략 1: ×2를 보면 → 같은 수를 두 번 더한다 (a + a). 전략 2: ×4를 보면 → 더블의 더블 (a → 2a → 4a). 전략 3: 분배법칙 — 4 × 7 = (2 × 7) + (2 × 7) = 14 + 14 = 28. 전략 4: 미지수 — 2 × ? = 14 → ?는 14의 절반 = 7. 검증: 짝수 × 짝수 = 짝수, 짝수 × 홀수 = 짝수 (×2와 ×4의 답은 항상 짝수).

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.4 Lesson 4.1 'Multiply with 2 and 4' pp.139-142
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic D & Module 3 — Multiplying with Units of 2 and 4
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

hints: Doubling means adding the number to itself: 9 + 9.
Or skip count by 2s nine times: 2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12, 14, 16, 18.

feedback.incorrect: Close, but 2 × 9 means 9 doubled. 9 + 9 = 18, so the answer is C.

try.question: 4 × 5 = ? (Use double the double.)

feedback.correct: Great — double the double works every time for ×4.
feedback.incorrect: Try this path: 2 × 5 = 10, then double 10 to get 20. Answer is C.

try.feedback_correct: Great — double the double works every time for ×4.

try.question: There are 2 rows of 7 chairs. How many chairs?
hints: 2 rows of 7 is the same as 2 × 7.

feedback.incorrect: 2 rows of 7 means 7 + 7 = 14. Answer is C.

try.question: Maya has 4 photo albums. Each album holds 8 pictures. How many pictures total?

feedback.incorrect: Break it down: 2 × 8 = 16, then double to get 32. Answer is C.

try.question: ? × 4 = 24. What is the missing factor?
hints: Skip count by 4s: 4, 8, 12, 16, 20, 24 — that's six fours.

feedback.incorrect: Count by 4s until you reach 24. You land at 24 on the sixth count. Answer is C.

practice_r2.question: True or False: 4 × 6 equals 2 × 6 plus 2 × 6.
hints: So 4 sixes equal 2 sixes plus 2 more sixes.

feedback.correct: Right — splitting 4 into 2 + 2 keeps the product the same.
feedback.incorrect: 2×6 + 2×6 = 12 + 12 = 24, which matches 4×6. The statement is True.

practice_r2.feedback_correct: Right — splitting 4 into 2 + 2 keeps the product the same.

practice_r2.question: Find the difference: 504 − 167.
practice_r2.explanation: 504−167: 4−7 borrow → 14−7=7; tens 0−6 borrow → 10−7=3 (after lending); hundreds 4−1=3. Result: 337.
hints: Borrow across the zero in tens.
